mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate during a financial crisis signals a liquidity trap and deteriorating fundamentals rather than stimulative easing, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate environments are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of instances when the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and rising unemployment indicate a broad-based economic contraction that compresses corporate earnings, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between industrial production and unemployment loses statistical significance (p-value greater than 0.10) in out-of-sample regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in retail sales signals resilient consumer demand and stabilizing corporate revenues, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative alpha during recovery phases or fails to generate positive returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in consumer price index growth below 0.5 percent month-over-month signals abating inflation pressures, allowing equity risk premium compression and predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain negative despite disinflation, or inflation rebounds sharply within the 20-day horizon, negating the risk premium compression effect.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts combined with falling real GDP indicate a structural collapse in aggregate demand and financial leverage, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing and GDP declines fail to predict equity drawdowns, or the correlation between these demand indicators and market returns breaks down in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in nonfarm payrolls directly reduces aggregate household income and corporate earnings expectations, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll declines are accompanied by positive equity returns in greater than 60 percent of cases, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Stabilization or a rebound in the index of industrial production signals supply chain normalization and inventory restocking, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production rebounds fail to generate positive 5-day returns, or the signal is consistently dominated by concurrent monetary policy shocks.
